title: Level 857
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:a world in darkness, devoid of hope}} {{CSS|content= /* Page */ .page { border: solid 6px rgba(255, 255, 255, 1); margin-top: 12px; position: relative; border-radius: 0px; box-shadow: 0px 0px 0px 0 rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.4); } /* Darkmode CSS */ .theme-fandomdesktop-dark { --theme-page-background-color: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.8); --theme-page-background-color--secondary: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.24); --theme-link-color: rgba(255, 0, 0, 1); --theme-border-color: black; .page-side-tool { border: solid 2px grey; box-shadow: 0px 0px 5px color white; } --theme-community-header-color: white; --theme-sticky-nav-background-color: black; --theme-sticky-nav-text-color: white; --theme-sticky-nav-text-color--hover: white; } /* Lightmode CSS */ .theme-fandomdesktop-light { --theme-page-background-color: rgba(235, 235, 235, 0.8); --theme-page-background-color--secondary: rgba(111, 111, 111, 0.3); --theme-link-color: rgba(166, 91, 185, 1); --theme-border-color: color here; .page-side-tool { border: solid 2px color here; box-shadow: 0px 0px 5px color here; } --theme-community-header-color: color here; --theme-sticky-nav-background-color: color here; --theme-sticky-nav-text-color: color here; --theme-sticky-nav-text-color--hover: color here; }}} {{CSS|content= html .resizable-container, .page-content h1, .page-content h2, .page-content h3, .page-content h4, .page-content h5, .page-content h6 { font-family:Barlow}} {{CSS|content= @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Barlow:ital,wght@0,100;0,200;0,300;0,400;0,500;0,600;0,700;0,800;0,900;1,100;1,200;1,300;1,400;1,500;1,600;1,700;1,800;1,900&display=swap'); }}
enable page ambience for best experience:{{Audio|round=yes|file=Pressure - The Walls Are Painted With Blood.mp3|align=center|loop=true|volume=0.8}}
'''
LEVEL 857 // "DESERTION"
'''
[[File:1130-2.gif|2000px]]
{{Blurquote|image=858 sublevel main.jpg|quote-text=
As much as these beasts might differ from the animals we were used to, and whether they had been reborn under the invisible and ruinous rays of sunlight, turned from inoffensive representatives of urban fauna into the spawn of hell, or whether they had always dwelled in the depths, only now to be disturbed by man - still, they were an evident part of life here. Disfigured, perverted - but a part of life here all the same. And they remained subject to that very same driving impulse known to every organic thing on [[The Frontrooms|this planet.]]


Survive. Survive at any cost.
|person=Dmitry Glukhovsky, Metro 2033}}



{{Class 5|hexagon=☣|color-h=42|color-s=35%|color-l=13%|class=Deadzone|safety=Strictly Uninhabitable|security=Unknown Information|entity=Predominantly Carcinogenic Hazards}}
'''Level 857''' is accessed by ████ █████████ ██ ████ █████ ███. It is recognized as one of the most environmentally hazardous levels throughout the Backrooms while still managing to sustain life.
OVERVIEW
{{Underline|FFFFFF}} [[File:858 ACTUAL orang.jpg|450x450px|center|link=]]
'''Level 857,''' otherwise known as '''"Desertion",''' is an interlinking set of dilapidated metro stations and tunnels entrenched in a hazy black smog. Perpetually lit emergency alarms scattered throughout the level illuminate the halls, casting an eerie orange glow throughout areas not otherwise devoid of lighting. The environment and labelling of stations in the level indicates the architecture to be of Russian descent, potentially stemming from a nonexistent city residing near Moscow. '''Level 857's''' underground section is in a dimmed state, requiring one to be adequately equipped with illumination gear to be efficient in navigation. This, along with the uneven terrain and unstable flooring, proves finding one's footing in '''Level 857''' to be a significant challenge.
'''Level 857's''' metro lines are cluttered with an abundance of discarded junk items, such as scrap metal, torn machinery, and miscellaneous materials like wooden boards or plastic products. Though certain sections may appear at first to be free of clutter, these areas will inevitably be flooded by further numbers of the aforementioned discarded items via an unknown source. As such, using the metro lines themselves as navigation throughout the level is entirely infeasible. Additionally, no trains - whether broken or not - have been discovered in any location throughout the stations or metro lines.
Due to the presence of a benzene-producing mold, '''Level 857''' is notorious for its pervasive carcinogenic hazards. A pale yellow haze wafts through the disrepaired ventilation systems, cycling it through the level and spreading its chemicals throughout the air. The abundance of benzene vapor in '''Level 857's''' air consequently adds a sweet, gasoline-like odor present in nearly all locations. In tandem with the putrid stench of burnt skin and meat, the level's stench commonly comes off as irritating and disorienting. Surviving wanderers who have explored '''Level 857''' have often returned with deformed, jaundiced skin which inevitably develops rudimentary tumors, typically growing calcified bony thorns along its surface in an excruciating process.

Most do not fully recover from this infection; that is if they are lucky enough to survive it.
Sub-Area // The Inconspicuous Internals
[[File:I-aint-showin-that.png|450x450px|center|link=]]
'''The Inconspicuous Internals''' is a sub-area of '''Level 857,''' accessed by entirely submerging oneself into the heap of junk that comprises the metro lines. '''The Inconspicuous Internals''' is composed of a constant, infinite space filled with the aforementioned discarded material, possessing an ochre concrete floor and clutter expanding in all other directions. Material in '''the Inconspicuous Internals''' includes stained packaging products, scrap metal, papery pulp, and unidentified clusters of petrochemical solids. A slimy, pervasive mucus coats the entirety of '''the Inconspicuous Internals,''' dampening the material of the level. Though the mucus' acidity has removed the presence of molds which produce carcinogenic byproducts, this property also slowly melts off the primary skin layers of wanderers which find themselves in this area, being compared to gastric acid (pH 1.8-2.1). One can exit '''the Inconspicuous Internals''' by swimming upwards after touching the sub-area's floor. A glaring cyan light will appear; swimming away from the light will result in one emerging from the top of a debris clutter in the main area metro lines of '''Level 857.''' This may take multiple attempts before one appears at a station in which they can exit said pile.
Sub-Area // Lavender Stations
[[File:Lavenderstation.jpg|450x450px|center|link=]]
'''Lavender Stations''' are small, dimly-lit rooms that seldom appear in place of regular stations in '''Level 857.''' '''Lavender Stations''' are characterized by their vaguely purple lighting and lack of yellowed fog - making a striking presence among the dreary tunnels. The reason for this is presumed to be a property of the light which possesses a form of "opposing field" against benzene, best likened to the way magnets of the same polarization act against each other. Nonetheless, despite this property, '''Benzenic Mold''' is still present in these areas, albeit in far lower amounts. Thus, the mold only poses a hazard in '''Lavender Stations''' when inhaled or consumed.

In terms of furniture, '''Lavender Stations''' are abundant in folding tables, commonly featuring dispenser jugs filled with an uncontaminated "tea" made from the pulp of mushrooms. This tea is safe to drink; however, it is advised to check for toxic mildews clogging the dispenser taps before use.
Certain portions of '''Level 857's''' metro are known to be physically disconnected and isolated from others; thus are only able to be accessed via trekking the surface. Concrete stairwells are littered uncommonly across the stations, which serve as transitory paths from the underground section to the surface. While these stairwells may be blocked by debris and rubble on odd occasions, if one manages to access the surface, they will be met with an immediate, searing radiation that pulses across the darkened wasteland.
{{Blurquote|image=Irradiated surface.jpg|quote-text=
"In pursuit of an escape from this oh-so-hostile environment, I made the crucial mistake of setting foot onto its surface. Immediately, I felt the blistering waves of a ruthless air ricocheting through my skin. I turned back into the tunnels and did not think for a second to return to that fucking hellhole of a wasteland since.
|person=Unknown Explorer A}} ---- [[File:Irradiated surface.jpg|450x450px|center|link=]]
The surface of '''Level 857''' composes of an endlessly stretching wintry plain with the faint etching of a road surrounding the perimeter. The "snow" of the surface's ground, consisting of an unidentified fine grey silicate, poses a noticeable threat to the lungs if inhaled. Due to the minimal wind activity in '''Level 857,''' however, little of this "snow" is scattered throughout the air. Tall, tree-like structures fill the areas beyond the road, appearing to mimic pine and fir trees with painted metallic trunks and clusters of steel wool as leaves. No biological presence is known to permanently reside on the surface, though certain molds in the underground section thrive from its radiative energy.
Level '''857's''' sky is entirely devoid of celestial bodies or natural lighting. As such, visibility on the surface is extremely limited; even if one brings along artificial lighting, the radiation possesses electric anomalies that allow it to suppress the flow of electricity in its bounds, causing the light to flicker out within just a few seconds of being activated. Products of chemical reactions such as fire are the only reliable method of lighting on the surface. The source of this anomalous radiation is believed to be caused by Level '''857's''' atmosphere - or rather, lack thereof. '''Level 857's''' atmosphere appears to be "torn" out of place, revealing an area beyond its bounds surpassing [[The Void|the Void.]] This space, referred to as the Uncharted Territory, encompasses both the Backrooms and [[The Frontrooms|the Frontrooms.]]
ECOSYSTEM
{{Underline|FFFFFF}}
'''Level 857''' is home to various entities found throughout damp unhygienic levels of the Backrooms, such as [[Entity 3|Smilers,]] [[Spines|Spines,]] [[Needlelimbs|Needlelimbs,]] [[Foams|Foams,]] [[Entity 44|Silver Slimes,]] rarely [[Mushroom Maw|Mushroom Maws,]] and more. Aside from Mushroom Maws, all entities not native to the level are affected by a phenomenon which causes them to act in a defensive manner rather than an aggressive manner - only resorting to such when threatened. '''Level 857''' features its own ecosystem of native life, albeit remaining less active and more self-sustaining.
{{Collapse|num=1|initial=
View "Benzenic Mold"
|final=
Close "Benzenic Mold"
|body=
Benzenic Mold
[[File:Benzenic mold.jpg|450x450px|center|link=]]
[[File:Benzenic mold formula.png|650x650px|center|link=]]
'''Benzenic Mold''' is a fungal infection that plagues the entirety of '''Level 857's''' underground; and is the major cause for the level's carcinogenic hazards. '''Benzenic Mold''' takes the appearance of clustery black patches which reside en-masse in the upper corners of '''Level 857's''' studded ceilings and throughout junk piles.
'''Benzenic Mold's''' biological process, dubbed "radiosynthesis," composes of utilizing the Uncharted Territory's radiation to convert carbon dioxide and water vapor into benzene vapor and oxygen gas. Benzene is majorly a by-product of '''Benzenic Mold's''' process (hence its namesake), while primarily utilizing oxygen and organic debris scattered about the walls to grow and expand to further regions. This organic debris primarily consists of the spores and proteins of mushrooms present throughout '''Level 857.'''
'''Benzenic Mold''' utilizes two methods of reproduction; either separating a part of itself to grow into a new nearby cluster, or releasing spores that float throughout the air to locate a further spot for more sufficient resources. '''Benzenic Mold''' deciding its reproduction method depends on its current environment, which can be used depending on the amount of materials which the mold can access in their current location. This broad coverage of several environments allows '''Benzenic Mold''' to be exceptionally adaptable; thus being a prominent presence throughout nearly all of '''Level 857.'''
}} {{Collapse|num=2|initial=
View "Mossworms"
|final=
Close "Mossworms"
|body=
Mossworms
[[File:Mossyboy.jpg|450x450px|center|link=]]
'''Mossworms''' are small, parasitic serpentine entities of a majorly ochre hue, prominently featuring a needlelike proboscis at its anterior. The oldest live '''Mossworms''' found, presumably in the juvenile stage, reach 8-9 inches in length from head to scut. With a broad, flat tail and two fin-like appendages, '''Mossworms''' spend most of their lifetimes swimming in the murky waters of '''Level 857's''' effluent pools. '''Mossworms''' possess three eyes above their proboscis, as well as being adorned with two whiskers for improved navigation in dense waste clusters. The tip of their proboscis features two pincers to latch onto skin and serrated sides presumably for wriggling beneath the skin. As pupae, '''Mossworms''' are more capsule-like in shape, with their proboscises being feathery in texture and serving as filter feeding tools. Pupa '''Mossworms''' possess a fatty outer layer for conserving nutrients, additionally serving as an adaptation state to the level's carcinogenic hazards. Mossworms typically remain in the pupa stage for ~4 weeks before transitioning into juveniles in which they grow a more wormlike body and develop a needle-shaped mouth for feeding in place of its particle filtering system. Adult forms of '''Mossworms''' are incredibly uncommon, either being extremely secluded or post-mortem in the main halls.
As mentioned previously, juvenile '''Mossworms''' primarily follow a parasitic feeding technique, using their pincers and serrated sides to enter the hosts body; typically of wanderers, though parasitism of [[Mushroom Maw|Mushroom Maws]] and [[Entity 5|Clumps]] has additionally been reported. Before acquiring a host, '''Mossworms''' will squirm among the upper corners of the underground - especially in large patches of moss, hence the name. Prior to finding a host, '''Mossworms''' will feed on the cellulose found within dense fibers of moss, though this method has a considerable lack of nutrition compared to the method in which they parasitize their hosts. Upon spotting a potential host, Mossworms use their tails to propel themselves from a moss cluster, using their strong pincers to latch onto the skin firmly. Their serrated sides assist in breaking the skin to squirm underneath, thrashing around upon latching to create a pocket which they can dig into. From this point, there is typically no hope for one being assailed by a '''Mossworm.'''
Once inside the skin, '''Mossworms''' navigate towards the vital organs, notably choosing the esophagus to break into. The serrated sides will then hook into the flesh, firmly securing themselves to the inner esophagus, where the '''Mossworm''' will complete the process of infecting a host, often referred to as "muscovermasis" by biologists within the Backrooms. The '''Mossworm''' will then secrete an acidic sludge from the end of its proboscis which liquefies small portions of the inner esophagus; this being the main method they parasitize a host. Additionally, '''Mossworms''' are also able to feed on food matter which the host consumes, similarly to parasitic isopods within the [[The Frontrooms|Frontrooms.]] Once a considerable majority of the esophagus has been melted, '''Mossworms''' begin their reproduction cycle inside the host; contrasting against the majority of [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Flatworm platyhelminths.] Once its host dies, '''Mossworms''' lay their eggs in several small clusters, with each batch containing ~1500 eggs - only about 30 eggs for each cluster will reach beyond pupae, however. '''Mossworms''' born inside hosts will exhibit more scavenger-like diets than parasitic diets, majorly feeding on the dead host. One wanderer can typically sustain '''Mossworms''' for several generations.
}} =====
PHENOMENA
===== {{Underline|FFFFFF}} {{Collapse|num=3|initial=
View "Defensivity"
|final=
Close "Defensivity"
|body=
"Defensivity"
'''Defensivity''' refers to the behavioral impact majorly seen on invasive entities found within '''Level 857.''' While it is unknown what causes this phenomenon to manifest, theories suggest that an entity's anomalous properties render it subject to rapid behavioral change in biological-based levels - especially when in close proximity with the Uncharted Territory. Entities affected by '''Defensivity''' will exhibit behavioral patterns of defensive priority rather than active aggression towards entities and wanderers. This trait will typically lead to the progressive weakening of the entity's strength if the affected entity is unable to capture sufficient prey, eventually causing it to "starve" and decease. The cause of the entity gaining such mimics of biological processes is majorly unknown as of now.
}}
DOCUMENTATION
{{Underline|FFFFFF}}
{{Collapse|num=4|initial=
Open Log #1: "Requietionis.pdf"
|final=
Close Log #1
|body=
"I tell 'ya, those Membrumitor things are vicious. Last time I wound up in '''Level 857,''' I spotted some fresh [[Object 1|Almond Water]] sitting right by a Membrumitor's corpse. Or, rather, I thought it was dead at first. Turns out, it was playing dead and almost tore my face clean off. If I could tell you one thing if you ever end up there:

"If it looks weak, it wants you to think it's weak."
> "Requietionis.pdf" Editor's Note: The entity (Requietionis Membrumitor) mentioned in this log is believed to be a species yet to be fully documented. Studies will continue on this subject.
~ Unknown Explorer B
}}
{{Collapse|num=5|initial=
Open Log #2: "TorpidEcosystem.pdf"
|final=
Close Log #2
|body=
"Sure, whatever, all these class-fives infested with entities might be scary when you first see them. But nothing truly prepares you for the deadliness that is Level 857's ecosystem; it's way less than that in the worst way possible. This mold, present throughout nearly the whole level, passively produces heaps of benzene as a byproduct, sending it right into the air and polluting it with a cancerous aura. And because it's so adaptable, it sticks around everywhere. Even after killing its unintentional victims, it simply uses the cancerous body to expand its reach even further. Imagine stumbling upon this level, not knowing any method of exiting, and eventually succumbing to your own carcinogenic tumors that weaken you to your grave. This ecosystem thrives on torpidity."
~ Unknown Explorer A
}}
ENTRANCES & EXITS
{{Underline|FFFFFF}}
'''Level 857''' is accessed by undocumented means from the underground networks of [[Level 338|Level 338.]] Likewise, if one so reasonably wishes to avoid such entrances, remaining far from isolated and ruined areas in [[Level 338]] will ensure one's abstinence from entrance to this level.
One can exit this level by locating a heavily brightened room located deep within exceptionally narrow halls. As one basks in the light for sufficient time, they will be returned to [[Level 858]] or [[Level 207|Level 207]]. Additionally, approaching the surface of the level might send one to [[Level 908]] instead.
{{Collapse|num=6|initial=Beyond the irradiated landscapes and desperate life...|final=...a glint of hope shimmers in the distance.|body= Author: [[User:Lemonmuffinn|Lemon]]
Inspired by Dmitry Glukhovsky's "Metro 2033"
